UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents the editable profile information for a user within the VoyageFlow application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This typically corresponds to the user's ID from the external authentication system.

email.description: The user's email address, primarily used for communication. Managed by the external authentication system.

addressStreet.description: The street address of the user.

addressCity.description: The city of the user's address.

addressPostalCode.description: The postal code of the user's address.

addressCountry.description: The country of the user's address.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

Booking.title: Booking
Booking.description: Stores details about a user's confirmed travel booking, acting as a container for individual booking items.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Booking entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made this booking. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Booking)

bookingReference.description: A unique, human-readable reference code generated for this booking, often used for external lookups and customer service.

bookingDate.description: The date and time when the booking was successfully confirmed and completed.

status.description: The current status of the entire booking (e.g., 'Confirmed', 'Pending', 'Cancelled', 'Completed').

totalAmount.description: The grand total monetary amount of the booking, combining all booking items.

currency.description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the total amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'NGN').

paymentId.description: A unique transaction identifier provided by the payment gateway (e.g., Paystack) for this booking's payment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the booking record was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the booking record was last updated.

BookingItem.title: BookingItem
BookingItem.description: Represents a specific travel service (e.g., a flight segment, a hotel stay, an activity) included within a larger Booking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BookingItem entity.

bookingId.description: Reference to the parent Booking this item belongs to. (Relationship: Booking 1:N BookingItem)

itemType.description: The type of travel service booked for this item (e.g., 'Flight', 'Hotel', 'Activity', 'Package').

name.description: A concise, descriptive name for the booked item (e.g., 'Morning Flight to Paris', 'Hilton Room - 3 Nights').

description.description: A detailed description of the booked item, including specifics like airline, flight number, hotel name, room type, activity details.

unitPrice.description: The price of a single unit of this booking item before quantity multiplication.

quantity.description: The number of units booked for this item (e.g., number of flight tickets, number of nights for a hotel, number of activity participants).

totalItemPrice.description: The total monetary amount for this specific booking item (unitPrice multiplied by quantity).

startDate.description: The start date and time of the service (e.g., flight departure, hotel check-in, activity start).

endDate.description: The end date and time of the service (e.g., flight arrival, hotel check-out, activity end).

locationDetails.description: Specific geographical or venue details related to the service (e.g., airport codes, hotel address, activity meeting point).

externalReferenceId.description: An identifier from the external service provider (e.g., airline PNR, hotel reservation number) for this specific item.

serviceProvider.description: The name of the external company providing the service (e.g., 'Air France', 'Hilton Hotels', 'Kayak Tours').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the booking item record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the booking item record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile data, accessible only by the owning user. The 'id' field within the document will match the 'userId' path parameter for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Collection of bookings made by a specific user. The 'userId' field in the document denormalizes ownership for authorization independence, ensuring that security rules do not require a 'get()' operation on the parent user document. Supports secure list operations.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this booking.

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific booking.

definition.description: Collection of individual items (e.g., flights, hotels) within a specific booking. The 'userId' field is denormalized from the grandparent user document for authorization independence. This eliminates the need for 'get()' operations on parent documents within security rules and supports secure list operations.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this booking item's parent booking.

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent booking.

params.name: bookingItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific booking item.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust, debuggable security rules and efficient queries. 

1.  **UserProfile (`/users/{userId}/profile`):** User profiles are stored as subcollections under a top-level `/users` collection. Each user's profile document is uniquely identified by their Firebase Authentication UID (`userId`), which is explicitly part of the path. This leverages **Path-Based Ownership (C.1)**, making authorization trivial: `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}` in the path. The `UserProfile.id` field will also mirror the `userId` in the path, reinforcing ownership and achieving **Authorization Independence (A)** without any `get()` calls.

2.  **Bookings (`/users/{userId}/bookings/{bookingId}`):** Bookings are also user-specific and follow a hierarchical path within the user's data. Each booking document resides under `/users/{userId}/bookings/{bookingId}`. The `Booking.userId` field is explicitly included in the document, which **denormalizes** the owner information. This denormalization is crucial for **Authorization Independence (A)**; rules for individual `Booking` documents can directly check `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` without needing to perform a `get()` on the parent user document. This also perfectly supports **QAPs (Query as a security Policy - D.2)** for listing, as a user can only query their own `bookings` subcollection (`/users/{request.auth.uid}/bookings`), and the `userId` in the path ensures this scope.

3.  **BookingItems (`/users/{userId}/bookings/{bookingId}/bookingItems/{bookingItemId}`):** Booking items are nested under their respective bookings, continuing the hierarchical structure. To maintain **Authorization Independence (A)** for `BookingItem` documents, the `userId` from the grandparent `User` is **denormalized** and included directly within each `BookingItem` document (i.e., `BookingItem.userId`). This is a critical design choice: it prevents rules from having to `get()` the parent `Booking` document or the grandparent `User` document to verify ownership. The security rule for `BookingItem` can simply check `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid`, making it atomic and highly performant. This structure also supports **QAPs**, allowing a user to securely list their own booking items for a specific booking (`/users/{request.auth.uid}/bookings/{bookingId}/bookingItems`) because the `userId` in the path and within the document ensures all items belong to the authenticated user.
